element_introduction_order.element: Basic flight controls and Newtonian physics
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: player must learn momentum-based movement; turning has no immediate effect until momentum is counteracted
learning profitable trade routes, price comparison across systems, calculating overhead costs (fuel, missiles, repair)
food, computer parts establish baseline trading before illegal goods introduced

element_introduction_order.element: Illegal trading goods (narcotics, nerve gas, weapons, slaves)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: high-profit contraband with risk of fines and police escalation; player chooses risk tolerance
handles routine intrasystem travel; player can delegate day-to-day navigation tasks
jump mechanics, maximum range calculation based on ship mass, visible hyperspace clouds enable pirate tracking
realistic physics-based combat with slingshot thrusts and laser exchanges; enemies appear more frequently in anarchic systems
Federation and Empire have independent rank systems; playing both factions increases difficulty; combat rating progresses from 'Harmless' to 'Elite'

element_introduction_order.element: System government types and danger levels
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core worlds are safest; anarchic systems (Riedquat, Phekda) are most hazardous; safety determines piracy risk
Federation vs Empire cold-war tension; player can choose any alignment or remain neutral; affects faction standing

typical_tension_triggers: carrying illegal cargo across policed systems
engaging in combat with enemy ships
traveling through hyperspace (vulnerable to pirates at exit point)
low fuel or resources in hostile territory
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: open-ended sandbox with self-directed tension; long intrasystem travel spans require time acceleration; tension cycles depend on player choices (legal vs illegal trading, faction engagement, system safety selection)

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: anarchic systems offer optional high-risk trading and combat; Core worlds provide optional safety
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: hyperspace clouds visible for hours after jumps, making player position traceable by pirates; indicates hidden chase/ambush mechanic

difficulty_curve.early_game: player inherits 100 credits and basic Eagle Long Range Fighter; must earn money through trading or missions; Newtonian physics create learning curve for flight control
difficulty_curve.mid_game: illegal trading introduces risk/reward decisions; faction military missions offer progression; player encounters pirates in hostile systems; combat rating system gates advancement from 'Harmless' upward
difficulty_curve.late_game: reaching 'Elite' combat rating; managing dual-faction military ranks; profitable routes become established; anarchic system exploration and piracy become viable
notable_spikes: first pirate encounter (introduces realistic combat physics under pressure)
first illegal cargo trade (risk escalation: fines → police attack)
traveling to anarchic systems like Riedquat or Phekda (hazard shift from safe Core worlds)
first hyperspace jump (introduction of tracking vulnerability)

notable_level_patterns.description: player selects profitable route (e.g., Barnard's Star to Sol), buys low, sells high, accounts for fuel/repair overhead; repeatable economic pattern with risk scaling based on cargo legality
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Barnard's Star – Sol route (documented profitable example)

notable_level_patterns.description: Core worlds (safest) → frontier systems (moderate) → anarchic systems (most dangerous); player selects engagement level based on risk tolerance and profit incentive
player jumps to new system, visible hyperspace cloud lingers; pirates can arrive ahead and ambush without police intervention; adds delayed tension to travel
notable_level_patterns.example_level: implicit mechanic across all interstellar transitions

notable_level_patterns.description: player completes freelance military missions for Federation or Empire; each combat victory counts toward rank; independent rank tracks create incentive specialization vs dual-play difficulty
notable_level_patterns.example_level: faction mission availability tied to player location and allegiance
